description: Extraction schema for SEC 10-K (annual) and 10-Q (quarterly) reports. These are large narrative documents (300K-500K text chars typical). Extractor chunks by section (Item 1 Business, Item 1A Risk Factors, Item 2 Properties, Item 7 MD&A) and merges results.

filing_metadata.fiscal_period_end: ISO date (the as-of date for the financials)

business_overview.description: 2-3 paragraph overview of what the company does, principal segments, geographic footprint, and key end markets
primary_segments: array of segment names as named in the filing
geographic_focus: array of regions: North America, South America, EMEA, APAC

segments.name: as named in filing (e.g. 'Ag Services and Oilseeds')

facilities_named.name: facility name as cited
facilities_named.country: US, BR, AR, CN, etc. (ISO-2 if possible)
facilities_named.facility_type: oilseed_crush | ethanol | biodiesel | renewable_diesel | grain_handling | rail_terminal | river_terminal | nutrition | feed_mill | beverage | flavors | corporate | r_and_d | other | unknown
facilities_named.operating_status: active | idle | closed | under_construction | announced | unknown
facilities_named.capacity_unit: string (bu/day, mgy, mt/year, sq_ft, etc.) or null
facilities_named.context: 1 sentence from filing — why mentioned, any operational notes

risk_factors_top.title: short title (e.g. 'Commodity price volatility')
risk_factors_top.category: macro | commodity | regulatory | operational | climate | geopolitical | financial | competitive | reputational | cyber | other

forward_guidance.metric: e.g. EPS, segment operating profit, capex

named_customers: array of customer names if disclosed

commodity_exposures.commodity: corn, soybeans, wheat, palm oil, etc.

policy_and_regulatory_callouts: Free-form list (e.g. RFS RVO, EU SAF mandate, Inflation Reduction Act 45Z, EPA Title V)
sections.extraction_notes: extractor caveats — sections that were truncated, quality flags, etc.

chunking_strategy.approach: Split by section using regex on '^Item N+[A-Z]?\.' pattern. Extract per chunk with focused prompt. Merge per-chunk results into the unified schema.
chunking_strategy.best_of_n_merge_strategy: For lists (facilities, risks): union by case-insensitive name match. For scalars (revenue): mode/median across passes. For sentiment: average.
